cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A medieval navigational instrument, refined by Muslim astronomers and adopted by European sailors, used to determine latitude by measuring the altitude of the sun or a star above the horizon

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A Chinese invention adopted in Europe during the Age of Exploration that uses a magnetized needle to point to true north, allowing ships to navigate accurately even when out of sight of land or stars

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A triangular sail of Arab origin that let ships sail effectively against the wind and was combined with European square sails on caravels and carracks

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A three- or four-masted sailing ship developed in 15th-century Portugal and Spain, large enough to haul cargo across oceans and used by Columbus and da Gama on their long voyages

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A small, fast, highly maneuverable Portuguese ship with lateen sails that enabled coastal exploration of Africa and transatlantic voyages in the 15th century

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A Dutch cargo ship of the late 16th century with a large hold and small crew requirement, making long-distance shipping cheaper and helping the Dutch dominate European shipping

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A large, multi-decked Spanish sailing ship used from the 16th to 18th centuries to carry silver from the Americas and as a warship in the Spanish Armada

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Global wind patterns such as the trade winds and westerlies that European navigators mapped and exploited to establish reliable transoceanic sailing routes

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Continuous flows of seawater such as the Gulf Stream whose knowledge allowed European captains to plan faster, more predictable voyages across the Atlantic and Pacific

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Portuguese royal who sponsored a school for navigators at Sagres and financed voyages down the West African coast in the early 15th century, launching Portugal's Atlantic empire

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Portuguese navigator who in 1488 became the first European to round the southern tip of Africa at the Cape of Good Hope, opening a sea route toward the Indian Ocean

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Portuguese captain who in 1498 completed the first sea voyage from Europe around Africa to India, establishing direct Portuguese access to Indian Ocean spice markets

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Genoese navigator funded by Spain whose 1492 voyage seeking a western route to Asia instead reached the Caribbean, initiating sustained European contact with the Americas

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Portuguese captain sailing for Spain whose 1519-1522 expedition completed the first circumnavigation of the globe, though he himself died in the Philippines

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Danish-born navigator in Russian service whose 1728 and 1741 voyages proved that Asia and North America were separated by the strait between Siberia and Alaska that now bears his name

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: British navigator whose late-18th-century Pacific voyages charted Australia, New Zealand, and Hawaii, bringing the last major oceanic frontier into European knowledge

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The dominance of one state or group over others in political, economic, or military affairs — the condition European powers sought to establish over Indian Ocean and Atlantic trade

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A network of fortified coastal outposts — pioneered by the Portuguese at Goa, Hormuz, and Malacca — used to control regional trade without conquering large inland territories

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 17th- and 18th-century struggle in which the Omani Sultanate expelled the Portuguese from East African ports such as Mombasa and reasserted regional control of Indian Ocean trade

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A complete voyage around the globe, first achieved by the remnants of Magellan's expedition in 1522, proving the Earth could be sailed as one continuous body of water

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A term for the original inhabitants of a region, used especially for the peoples of the Americas, Africa, and Oceania encountered — and often devastated — by European expansion

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: 1494 papal-brokered agreement that divided newly discovered non-Christian lands between Spain and Portugal along a line west of the Cape Verde Islands, giving Brazil to Portugal

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A Spanish soldier-explorer such as Cortes or Pizarro who led small armed expeditions to subjugate Indigenous empires in the Americas for the Spanish Crown

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A royal official appointed to govern a Spanish colonial territory such as New Spain or Peru in the name of the king, holding broad political and military authority

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Portuguese admiral who in 1510-1515 seized Goa, Malacca, and Hormuz, creating the fortified choke-points of Portugal's Indian Ocean trading post empire

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Portuguese captain whose 1500 expedition bound for India was blown westward and became the first European recorded contact with Brazil, which he claimed for Portugal

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Spanish co-monarchs who completed the Reconquista in 1492 and financed Columbus's voyage the same year, launching Spain's overseas empire

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Spanish conquistador who in 1513 crossed the Isthmus of Panama and became the first European to sight the Pacific Ocean from the Americas

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Spanish conquistador who in the 1530s captured the Inca emperor Atahualpa at Cajamarca and toppled the Inca Empire with a force of under 200 men

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Spanish conquistador who between 1519 and 1521 allied with rival Indigenous states to overthrow the Aztec Empire and seize Tenochtitlan for Spain

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Dominican friar who condemned the encomienda and the abuse of Indigenous peoples in his writings, advocating their rights before the Spanish Crown in the mid-16th century

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: French navigator whose 1530s voyages up the St. Lawrence River established France's claim to what would become Canada

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: English navigator who sailed for the Dutch and English in the early 1600s searching for a Northwest Passage, exploring the river and bay that bear his name

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An Old World virus that, spread by European contact, killed an estimated 50-90 percent of the Indigenous population of the Americas and became the deadliest single agent of conquest

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An American staple grain — corn — that was carried to Africa, Europe, and Asia after 1492, boosting food supplies and population growth across the Old World

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A Mesoamerican bean from which chocolate is made, exported from the Americas after Spanish conquest and transformed into a luxury beverage across Europe

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Crops such as sugar, tobacco, and cotton grown on large plantations primarily for export rather than local consumption, driving demand for coerced labor in the Americas

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Portuguese sugar mills in colonial Brazil that combined cane fields, crushing machinery, and enslaved African labor to produce refined sugar for European markets

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A bitter compound derived from Andean cinchona bark that Europeans adopted to treat malaria, enabling sustained European activity in tropical Africa and Asia

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A 1531 reported apparition of the Virgin Mary to an Indigenous convert near Mexico City whose image fused Catholic and Nahua symbolism and became the patroness of Mexican Catholic identity

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Founder of the Tokugawa shogunate in 1603 whose sakoku policies expelled most European missionaries and sharply restricted Christianity and foreign trade in Japan

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Accumulated wealth — money, ships, tools, land — invested to generate more wealth, whose growth in early modern Europe financed long-distance voyages and joint-stock companies

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 16th-18th century European shift toward a market economy driven by overseas trade, banking, insurance, and joint-stock companies

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The three-legged transatlantic trade network that carried manufactured goods from Europe to Africa, enslaved Africans to the Americas, and cash-crop commodities back to Europe

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The dominant early modern European economic theory that national power depended on accumulating bullion through a favorable balance of trade, driving tariffs, monopolies, and colonial extraction

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A business in which investors buy shares, pooling capital and sharing risk — the organizational form behind the British and Dutch East India Companies

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Exclusive control over a commodity or trade route granted by a government, such as the Dutch lock on Indonesian spices or the Spanish on American silver

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: West African kingdom that grew wealthy and militarized in the 17th-18th centuries by capturing and selling enslaved people to European traders on the Slave Coast

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Tokugawa Japan's 17th-century 'closed country' policy that banned most foreign trade and travel, permitting only limited Dutch and Chinese contact at Nagasaki

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A large Spanish colonial estate producing livestock or wheat for regional markets, typically worked by debt-peon laborers bound to the landowner

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Spanish colonial labor grant that gave a conquistador the right to demand tribute and forced labor from Indigenous villagers in exchange for nominally Christianizing them

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The forced shipment of roughly 12 million Africans to the Americas from the 16th through 19th centuries to work sugar, tobacco, and cotton plantations

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The brutal Atlantic leg of the triangle trade on which enslaved Africans endured weeks in the holds of slave ships, with mortality rates commonly reaching 15-25 percent

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Any labor system that compels work through force or legal compulsion rather than free wages — including encomienda, mit'a, chattel slavery, and indentured servitude

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Spanish American racial hierarchy that ranked colonial society by ancestry — peninsulares, criollos, mestizos, mulattoes, Indigenous, and Africans — determining legal and economic rights

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The hypothesized Arctic sea route through North America that English, French, and Dutch explorers sought in vain as an alternative to Spanish- and Portuguese-controlled southern routes

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Chartered in 1600, this joint-stock company dominated trade with South Asia, built a private army, and eventually ruled large parts of India until 1858

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Chartered in 1602, the world's first joint-stock company with its own military — it dominated the spice trade from Indonesia and controlled Batavia and Cape Town

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Chartered in 1621 to manage Dutch trade and colonization in the Atlantic, including New Netherland, Dutch Brazil, and a large share of the trans-Atlantic slave trade

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Joint-stock company founded by Colbert in 1664 to direct French commercial activity in India and the Indian Ocean, competing with its British and Dutch rivals

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Semi-autonomous Eastern Slavic warrior communities of the steppe who rose in major revolts against Russian serfdom and Romanov authority, including the 1773-75 uprising under Pugachev

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Hindu warrior confederacy in western India whose wars against Aurangzeb and later the British challenged both Mughal and European imperial power

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Central African kingdoms in present-day Angola that fought Portuguese slave raids for decades under Queen Nzinga in the 17th century

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A self-liberated African who escaped plantation slavery in the Caribbean or Americas and formed independent communities that often waged guerrilla war against colonial powers

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Jamaican Maroon settlement founded in the early 18th century by Queen Nanny that resisted British colonial forces and negotiated a 1740 treaty recognizing its autonomy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Small artificial island in Nagasaki harbor where the Tokugawa regime confined Dutch traders from 1641 onward as Japan's sole direct contact with Europe during sakoku

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: 17th-century queen of Ndongo and Matamba who waged a decades-long war and diplomatic campaign against Portuguese slave raiders in what is now Angola

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Maroon leader in early-18th-century Jamaica who founded Nannytown and led guerrilla resistance that forced the British to sign a treaty granting Maroon autonomy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Wampanoag sachem who in 1675-76 led a pan-Indigenous war against New England colonists, whose defeat opened southern New England to further English settlement

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Don Cossack who led the massive 1773-75 peasant and serf uprising against Catherine the Great's Russia, claiming to be the rightful Tsar Peter III

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Landowning but untitled elite class — for example, in Qing China and early modern England — that filled local offices and gained status through education, rents, and civil examinations

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Risk-taking merchants and investors who organized early modern long-distance trade, joint-stock ventures, and manufacturing enterprises

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: State officials, such as Qing scholar-gentry or Ottoman viziers, whose administrative power derived from office-holding rather than inherited noble rank

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A hereditary form of slavery in which enslaved people are legally treated as personal property that can be bought, sold, and inherited — the dominant form in the Atlantic plantation economy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Labor contract in which a worker agreed to serve a master for a fixed term — typically 4-7 years — in exchange for passage to the Americas, food, and shelter

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Period from roughly 1530 to 1656 when Ottoman royal women — most famously Hurrem Sultan — exercised unprecedented political influence over the sultan and imperial policy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Jews whose ancestors lived on the Iberian Peninsula and were expelled in 1492, forming diaspora communities in the Ottoman Empire, Netherlands, and the Americas that shaped early modern trade networks

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Ukrainian-born wife of Suleiman the Magnificent whose rise from enslaved concubine to legal wife and political adviser helped launch the Ottoman Sultanate of Women
